records.ety: [L. securus; pref. se- without + cura care. See Cure care, and cf. Sure, a.]

defs: Free from fear, care, or anxiety; easy in mind; not feeling suspicion or distrust; confident.
quotes: But thou, secure of soul, unbent with woes.

defs: Overconfident; incautious; careless; -- in a bad sense.
Confident in opinion; not entertaining, or not having reason to entertain, doubt; certain; sure; -- commonly with of; as, secure of a welcome.
quotes: Confidence then bore thee on, secure Either to meet no danger, or to find Matter of glorious trial.

defs: Not exposed to danger; safe; -- applied to persons and things, and followed by against or from.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Secured (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Securing.]

defs: To make safe; to relieve from apprehensions of, or exposure to, danger; to guard; to protect.
quotes: I spread a cloud before the victor's sight, Sustained the vanquished, and secured his flight.

defs: To put beyond hazard of losing or of not receiving; to make certain; to assure; to insure; -- frequently with against or from, rarely with of; as, to secure a creditor against loss; to secure a debt by a mortgage.
quotes: It secures its possessor of eternal happiness.

defs: To make fast; to close or confine effectually; to render incapable of getting loose or escaping; as, to secure a prisoner; to secure a door, or the hatches of a ship.
To get possession of; to make one's self secure of; to acquire certainly; as, to secure an estate.
